(*auxilium*), which included personal service, providing troops (raising levies), and later [scutage](/wiki/Scutage "Scutage") (a payment) in lieu of service. (*consilium*), which encompassed everything from security (being a guard) through rendering advice in council, providing [squires](/wiki/Squire "Squire") and even in some cases providing *[de facto](/wiki/De_facto "De facto")* hostages.

(aids), often called [feudal aids](/wiki/Feudal_aid "Feudal aid"), were monies due upon certain contingent events, such as contributing to the lord's ransom, or to pageant-like events at court such as royal marriages.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Obligations in a feudal system

| [English feudalism](/wiki/Feudalism_in_England "Feudalism in England") |
| --- |
| *[Harold Sacramentum Fecit Willelmo Duci](/wiki/Harold_Godwinson#In_northern_France "Harold Godwinson")* ([Bayeux Tapestry](/wiki/Bayeux_Tapestry "Bayeux Tapestry")) |
| * [Fief](/wiki/Fief "Fief") * [Ecclesiastical fief](/wiki/Ecclesiastical_fief "Ecclesiastical fief") * [Crown land](/wiki/Crown_land "Crown land") * [Allodial title](/wiki/Allodial_title "Allodial title") * [Appanage](/wiki/Appanage "Appanage") * [Vassal](/wiki/Vassal "Vassal") * [Feoffment](/wiki/Feoffment "Feoffment") * [Seignory](/wiki/Seignory "Seignory") * [Subinfeudation](/wiki/Subinfeudation "Subinfeudation") * [Feoffee](/wiki/Feoffee "Feoffee") * [Fealty](/wiki/Fealty "Fealty") * [Homage](/wiki/Homage_(feudal) "Homage (feudal)") * [Affinity](/wiki/Affinity_(medieval) "Affinity (medieval)") * [Feudal maintenance](/wiki/Feudal_maintenance "Feudal maintenance") * [Feudal fragmentation](/wiki/Feudal_fragmentation "Feudal fragmentation") * [Bastard feudalism](/wiki/Bastard_feudalism "Bastard feudalism") * [Livery](/wiki/Livery "Livery") |
| [Manorialism](/wiki/Manorialism "Manorialism") |
| * [Lord of the manor](/wiki/Lord_of_the_manor "Lord of the manor") * [Manorial court](/wiki/Manorial_court "Manorial court") * [Manor house](/wiki/Manor_house "Manor house") ([List](/wiki/List_of_manor_houses "List of manor houses")) * [Demesne](/wiki/Demesne "Demesne") * [Glebe](/wiki/Glebe "Glebe") * [Overlord](/wiki/Overlord "Overlord") * [Lord](/wiki/Lord "Lord") * [Peasant](/wiki/Peasant "Peasant") * [Serfdom](/wiki/Serfdom "Serfdom") * [Free tenant](/wiki/Free_tenant "Free tenant") |
| [Feudal land tenure in England](/wiki/Feudal_land_tenure_in_England "Feudal land tenure in England") |
| * [Land tenure](/wiki/Land_tenure "Land tenure") * [English feudal barony](/wiki/English_feudal_barony "English feudal barony") * [Feudal baron](/wiki/Feudal_baron "Feudal baron") * [Knight's fee](/wiki/Knight%27s_fee "Knight's fee") * [Knight-service](/wiki/Knight-service "Knight-service") * [Baronage](/wiki/Baronage "Baronage") * [Peerage](/wiki/Peerage "Peerage") * [Serjeanty](/wiki/Serjeanty "Serjeanty") * [Copyhold](/wiki/Copyhold "Copyhold") * [Freehold](/wiki/Freehold_(law) "Freehold (law)") * [Gavelkind](/wiki/Gavelkind "Gavelkind") * [Customary freehold](/wiki/Customary_freehold "Customary freehold") * [Landed gentry](/wiki/Landed_gentry "Landed gentry") * [Peerages in the United Kingdom](/wiki/Peerages_in_the_United_Kingdom "Peerages in the United Kingdom") |
| Feudal duties |
| * [Avera and inward](/wiki/Avera_and_inward "Avera and inward") * [Socage](/wiki/Socage "Socage") * [Scutage](/wiki/Scutage "Scutage") * [Feudal aid](/wiki/Feudal_aid "Feudal aid") * [Scot and lot](/wiki/Scot_and_lot "Scot and lot") * [Tallage](/wiki/Tallage "Tallage") |
| [Feudalism](/wiki/Feudalism "Feudalism") |
| * [v](/wiki/Template:English_feudalism "Template:English feudalism") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:English_feudalism "Template talk:English feudalism") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:English_feudalism "Special:EditPage/Template:English feudalism") |

**Feudal duties** were the set of reciprocal financial, military and legal obligations among the warrior nobility in a [feudal system](/wiki/Feudalism "Feudalism").[[1]](#cite_note-Ganshof-1) These duties developed in both Europe and Japan with the decentralisation of empire and due to lack of monetary liquidity, as groups of warriors took over the social, political, judicial, and economic spheres of the territory they controlled.[[2]](#cite_note-2) While many feudal duties were based upon control of a parcel of land and its productive resources, even landless [knights](/wiki/Knight "Knight") owed feudal duties such as direct military service in their [lord's](/wiki/Lord#Feudalism "Lord") behest. Feudal duties were not uniform over time or across political boundaries, and in their later development also included duties from and to the [peasant](/wiki/Peasant "Peasant") population, such as [abergement](/w/index.php?title=Abergement_(feudal_law)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Abergement (feudal law) (page does not exist)").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Feudal duties ran both ways, both up and down the feudal hierarchy; however, aside from distribution of land[[4]](#cite_note-4) and [maintenance](/wiki/Feudal_maintenance "Feudal maintenance") of landless retainers, the main obligation of the feudal lord was to protect his [vassals](/wiki/Vassal "Vassal"), both militarily from incursion and judicially via court justice.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) In addition to lands, the lord could grant what were called "immunities", but were rights to conduct governmental functions such as the collecting of taxes and tolls, the holding of judicial proceedings, and even the coinage of money.[[7]](#cite_note-7) In addition there were contingent duties the lord owed such as the duty to take back a fief that was rejected by an heir (*[droit de déguerpissement](/w/index.php?title=Droit_de_d%C3%A9guerpissement&action=edit&redlink=1 "Droit de déguerpissement (page does not exist)")*).[[8]](#cite_note-8) Sometimes, particularly in the Frankish kingdoms, a lord would grant a fief to an assemblage of men rather than to a single vassal. These grants were called *[bans](/wiki/Ban_(medieval) "Ban (medieval)")*[[9]](#cite_note-9) and included extensive governmental autonomy, or immunities.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Duties owed by a vassal to his lord can be categorised into four types:[[11]](#cite_note-Russelll-types-11)

* **Military** (*auxilium*), which included personal service, providing troops (raising levies), and later [scutage](/wiki/Scutage "Scutage") (a payment) in lieu of service. Military duties also included work on fortifications and roads and bridges, thus the *[trinoda necessitas](/wiki/Trinoda_necessitas "Trinoda necessitas")*.[[12]](#cite_note-12)
* **Court duties** (*consilium*), which encompassed everything from security (being a guard) through rendering advice in council, providing [squires](/wiki/Squire "Squire") and even in some cases providing *[de facto](/wiki/De_facto "De facto")* hostages.
* **Special taxes** (aids), often called [feudal aids](/wiki/Feudal_aid "Feudal aid"), were monies due upon certain contingent events, such as contributing to the lord's ransom, or to pageant-like events at court such as royal marriages.[[13]](#cite_note-13)
* **Incidents**, which included such things as a negotiated [kickback](/wiki/Kickback_(bribery) "Kickback (bribery)") to the lord upon being granted a fief (politely called a *receipt*), the duty to feed and house the lord and his retinue when the lord visited (*[droit de gîte](/w/index.php?title=Droit_de_g%C3%AEte&action=edit&redlink=1 "Droit de gîte (page does not exist)")*), allowing the lord to hunt or fish on his land (*[droit de garenne](/w/index.php?title=Droit_de_garenne&action=edit&redlink=1 "Droit de garenne (page does not exist)")*) and being subject to the residual lordly rights of guardianship upon minority inheritance, and forfeiture upon a failure of heirs or failure to observe his feudal obligations.[[11]](#cite_note-Russelll-types-11)

In Europe, church lands were also held with feudal duties. While some churchmen did provide direct military service, most either hired substitutes, paid scutage, or later converted the duty to one of prayer, [frankalmoin](/wiki/Frankalmoin "Frankalmoin").[[11]](#cite_note-Russelll-types-11)

## List

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Feudal_duties&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: List")]

Feudal duties included, but were not limited to:

### From the Vassals to their Lord

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Feudal_duties&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: From the Vassals to their Lord")]

* [Amober](/wiki/Amober "Amober") – fee paid to a lord on the marriage of a maiden in his manor
* [Appanage](/wiki/Appanage "Appanage") – concession of a fief by the sovereign to his younger sons
* [Avera and inward](/wiki/Avera_and_inward "Avera and inward") – feudal obligations assessed against a [royal demesne](/wiki/Crown_land "Crown land")
* [Carucage](/wiki/Carucage "Carucage") – land tax based on the size (variously calculated) of the taxpayer's estate
* [Castle-guard](/wiki/Castle-guard "Castle-guard") – provision of knights to guard royal castles
* [Chiefage](/wiki/Chiefage "Chiefage") – a [poll](/wiki/Poll_tax "Poll tax")-money paid to their lords by those who held lands
* [Corvée](/wiki/Corv%C3%A9e "Corvée") – unpaid, [unfree](/wiki/Forced_labour "Forced labour") intermittent labour for limited periods of time
* [Droit de gîte](/w/index.php?title=Droit_de_g%C3%AEte&action=edit&redlink=1 "Droit de gîte (page does not exist)") – duty to feed and house the lord and his retinue when the lord visited
* [Droit de garenne](/w/index.php?title=Droit_de_garenne&action=edit&redlink=1 "Droit de garenne (page does not exist)") – allowing the lord to hunt or fish on a [vassal](/wiki/Vassal "Vassal")'s land
* [Feudal aid](/wiki/Feudal_aid "Feudal aid") – payment to a lord on certain occasions, such as the knighting of his eldest son, marriage of his eldest daughter
* [Merchet](/wiki/Merchet "Merchet") – fee paid on a marriage
* [Scot and lot](/wiki/Scot_and_lot "Scot and lot") – local levies and their associated obligations and rights, such as for drainage
* [Scutage](/wiki/Scutage "Scutage") – a payment accepted as an alternative to knight-service
* [Taille](/wiki/Taille "Taille") – a direct land tax on the French peasantry and non-nobles
* [Tallage](/wiki/Tallage "Tallage") – an occasional payment exacted by king and barons

### From the Lord to their Vassals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Feudal_duties&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: From the Lord to their Vassals")]

* [Banalité](/wiki/Banalit%C3%A9 "Banalité") – [Public services](/wiki/Public_service "Public service") provided by the feudal lord (such as community ovens, mills, wine presses, etc.) in exchange for a tribute from the vassals for their economic maintenance.
* [Feudal maintenance](/wiki/Feudal_maintenance "Feudal maintenance") – money payment to soldiers fighting in the interest and at the command of their lord
* [Frankalmoin](/wiki/Frank_almoin "Frank almoin") – land held by an ecclesiastical body free of secular service, commonly in return for religious services.
* [Knight-service](/wiki/Knight-service "Knight-service") – tenure of land to a knight in return for military service for his overlord
* [Patronage](/wiki/Patronage "Patronage") – protection and support to a client, who owed loyalty and service in return
* [Socage](/wiki/Socage "Socage") – tenure of land and provision of certain services (such as protection) to the vassals in return for a specified duty (usually money) to the lord that were different to standard knight-service
* [Serjeanty](/wiki/Serjeanty "Serjeanty") – tenure in return for a specified duty other than standard knight-service
